metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5282
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1b: ARMS (gene KIDINS220) is detected in synaptic biochemical fractions from hippocampal neurons, co-migrating with Trk receptors and GluA1 (GRIA1) in various subfractions from P2.

Figure 1c: immunogold EM showed of hippocampal CA1 showed ARMS localized to endomembranes in the dendritic shaft and spine. No presynaptic localization was observed.

Figure 4: immunofluorescence imaging of 15DIV hippocampal neurons showed intracellular GluA1 co-localized with ARMS whereas surface GluA1 did not.. Experimental description: M&M: "Electron microscopy - Adult male mice or Sprague-Dawley rats were anesthetized "

antibody:
"rabbit polyclonal (Kong et al., 2001) and mouse monoclonal ARMS antibodies (gift of G. Schiavo)" PMID: 11150334

Figure 1 of this PMID that authors refer to for the ARMS antibody shows the protein sequence and mention this is GenBank accession BAA86564, confirming this is human gene KIDINS220

checks.label: OWL Consistency
checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
